title: GPU Parallelization
concept: Transformers process the entire input sentence at once during training, maximizing GPU utilization and accelerating training.

exercises.prompt: Which computer hardware is specifically designed to perform thousands of mathematical calculations at the same time?
exercises.explain: GPUs (Graphics Processing Units) are hardware chips designed to run thousands of math calculations simultaneously, making them perfect for modern AI.

cards.title: Supercharged Training
cards.body: Before Transformers, training AI on huge libraries of books took months. Why? Because older models read like humans do: one word at a time. Transformers changed the game by reading the entire page all at once, unlocking the massive parallel processing power of modern graphics cards (GPUs).

cards.title: What is GPU Parallelization?
cards.body: GPU Parallelization is the ability to run thousands of calculations at the exact same time using a graphics card. Because Transformers look at all words in a sentence simultaneously, they can hand the whole sentence to the GPU to process in parallel, rather than waiting word-by-word.

cards.title: The Laser Printer analogy
cards.body: Imagine typing a document letter-by-letter on an old typewriter versus printing the entire page all at once with a laser printer. RNNs are like the typewriter, processing one word after another. Transformers are like the laser printer, throwing the entire page onto the paper (the GPU) in a single flash!

cards.title: Transformers in Action
cards.body: If you train a model on the sentence 'The cat sat on the mat', an older RNN must process 'The', then 'cat', then 'sat', step-by-step. A Transformer feeds 'The cat sat on the mat' into the GPU all at once. The GPU calculates relationships between all these words simultaneously, finishing the training step in a fraction of the time.

cards.title: Myth: Less Data = More Speed?
cards.body: Many believe Transformers process text faster because they use less data. In fact, they use much more data! They are faster simply because they utilize GPU hardware efficiently by processing all inputs at the exact same time, rather than in a slow, sequential loop.

cards.title: Sequential vs. Parallel Training
cards.body: Let's look at how data flows through a GPU during training. Notice how the sequential approach creates a bottleneck, while the parallel approach uses the full power of the hardware.

left.text: Processes: Word 1 -> Word 2 -> Word 3. The GPU sits mostly idle waiting for the next step.

right.text: Processes: [Word 1, Word 2, Word 3] all at once. The GPU works at maximum capacity.

exercises.prompt: How do Transformers utilize the GPU differently during training compared to older AI models?

options.text: They process text one word at a time in a long chain.
They feed the entire sentence into the GPU at once.
They avoid using GPUs entirely to save power.
They only train on one sentence per day.

exercises.explain: Older models (like RNNs) processed words one by one, creating a bottleneck. Transformers process all words at once, which fits perfectly with how GPUs work.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Transformers train faster because they do not have to wait for one word to finish processing before starting the next.
exercises.explain: Yes! Processing the whole sentence at once allows the GPU to perform calculations in parallel, which is what makes training so fast.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to describe how Transformers process data.
exercises.sentence: Transformers train faster because they process all words in a sentence in _____ on the GPU.
exercises.explain: GPUs are built to do many calculations at the same time, which is called parallel processing. Transformers feed the entire sentence into the GPU at once to take advantage of this.

exercises.prompt: Which response represents how a Transformer handles input text during training?

options.text: \"I read the first word, memorize it, then move to the next word, slowly building the sentence structure.\"
\"I analyze all the words in the sentence at the same time using parallel calculations on the GPU.\"
\"I ignore the GPU and use the CPU to process words in alphabetical order.\"

exercises.explain: The AI assistant correctly explains that Transformers process all tokens simultaneously, allowing the GPU to run calculations in parallel. The human-like answer describes sequential (step-by-step) reading.

exercises.prompt: How do Transformers speed up training compared to older recurrent neural networks (RNNs)?

options.text: By translating sentences into different languages before training starts
By processing all words in a sentence simultaneously to utilize GPU parallel processing
By using smaller neural networks that require less computer memory
By running training steps one word at a time in a strict sequence

exercises.explain: Transformers excel at speed because their architecture allows them to process all tokens at once during training, maximizing the parallel computation power of modern GPUs. Recurrent networks, on the other hand, are bottlenecked by having to wait for the previous word to finish processing.
